latest.title: Application for Admission to Trading
latest.content:
Today 13:51
RNS Number : 4730D
Synthomer PLC
07 May 2026
Synthomer plc(the "Company")
Application for Admission of Further Securities to Trading
Further to the Company's announcement on 30 April 2026 of its final results for the year ended 31 December 2025 and the refinancing of its key debt facilities, the Company has allotted and issued 430,008 new ordinary shares of £0.01 each in the capital of the Company (representing approximately 0.26% of the Company's enlarged share capital following such issuance) (the "
New Ordinary Shares
") to certain of the Group's lenders in satisfaction of certain of the fees payable to them in connection with the refinancing.
An application has been made to London Stock Exchange plc ("
LSE
") for the New Ordinary Shares to be admitted to trading on the LSE's main market for listed securities ("
Admission
"). It is expected that Admission will become effective on or before 8.00 a.m. on 8 May 2026.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. latest.content:
Today 16:40
RNS Number : 6641C
Synthomer PLC
30 April 2026
Synthomer plc
2025 Annual Report and Accounts
Synthomer plc (the "Company") has today published and submitted the annual report and accounts for the year ended 31 December 2025 for publication to the National Storage Mechanism in accordance with Listing Rule 6.4.1.
This document will shortly be available for inspection in unedited full text at
https://data.fca.org.uk/#/nsm/nationalstoragemechanism
.
The annual report and accounts are also available to view on the Company's website,
www.synthomer.com
, or on request from the Company's Company Secretary at 10 Greycoat Place, London SW1P 1SB.
The Company expects to publish the notice of annual general meeting 2026 and related documents in due course, in advance of the shareholder meeting which will take place on 22 June 2026.
This announcement is made in accordance with DTR 6.3.5(1A).
Legal Entity Identifier (LEI): 213800EHT3TI1KPQQJ56.

company_30s.what_they_do: SYNT.L designs and sells synthetic financial products—structured notes, synthetic ETFs, and derivative contracts that give investors exposure to a range of underlying assets without the need to own them outright.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Synthetic products let traders gain diversified exposure at lower capital outlays, a growing niche as investors seek cost‑efficient ways to hedge or speculate. The company’s platform supports rapid creation of bespoke exposures, filling a gap left by traditional asset managers.
company_30s.current_state: After a recent admission to trading and a series of leadership changes, SYNT.L is in a rebuilding phase. The company has been restructuring debt, cutting costs, and pursuing new financing. It is still burning cash but has begun to stabilise its balance sheet. The share price is trading near the bottom of its 52‑week range.

rns_narrative: The company has been busy reshuffling its board and CFO, signalling a push to tighten governance.
A refinancing update indicates that SYNT.L is working to replace high‑cost debt with longer‑dated, lower‑interest notes.
Recent financial reports show modest revenue but a clear path to profitability once the debt structure is finalised.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silent but watching – the market is keeping its eyes on the boardroom.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A few traders see the synthetic exposure model as a long‑term growth engine, especially if the new financing stabilises liquidity.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others fear that the debt load and lack of clear revenue streams could push the company toward a forced sale or delisting.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has barely budged after the CFO change; a decisive announcement would shift the mood.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker are flat – no public search activity, which means a surprise catalyst could hit hard.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the refinancing package – a public announcement of debt restructuring would remove a major drag on the balance sheet.
Release of the next quarter’s earnings – any positive surprise in revenue or margin would validate the business model.
A volume surge at the 97.7p support level – a 30‑day moving‑average break above 10k shares would confirm that capitulation is over.
Regulatory approval for a new synthetic product line – expanding the product catalogue could drive demand.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a 20‑day average volume exceeding 25k shares, price touching the 97.7p support, and any mention of debt repayment in the next two months.
expect_next.timing_regime: The share appears to be in the early post‑crash phase; the APEX compression suggests a tight spring. Confirmation of mean reversion will come when the price holds the support and volume starts to climb.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Speculative Long – the depth of the crash and lack of public chatter make it a contrarian play if a catalyst materialises.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the share recovers to roughly 50% of its 52‑week high, that equates to a 70% upside from its current trough. The deep crash (70‑95%) historically produces large rebounds, and the absence of negative sentiment means the market is primed for surprise.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company’s liquidity risk remains high; if the refinancing falls through, the share could slide further. A continued 30‑day downtrend would wipe out the upside potential.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: ~70% if the price climbs to halfway of the 52‑week high. Potential downside: ~30% if the share continues to fall or if debt restructuring fails. The asymmetry is clearly in favour of a bullish stance.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a bounce off the 97.7p support with a tight stop 5p below that level. Allocate 5–10% of the discretionary portfolio to this trade and keep a 2:1 risk‑reward ratio in mind.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the company announces refinancing or until volume spikes 30% above the 20‑day average. Only then consider a partial entry.
playbook.if_passing: None identified; there is no evidence of imminent delisting or bankruptcy.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deep crash with no current chatter and a looming refinancing event creates a high‑asymmetry contrarian play – the stock could rebound sharply if the debt deal closes and volume confirms a support hold.
